task.title: Working with large partitioned data sets
task.shortdesc: To improve performance for large partitioned data sets, you can limit the number of members that are displayed in the Remote Systems view.

keywords.indexterm: Remote Systems viewworking with large partitioned data setspartitioned data setslarge

taskbody.prereq: To set preferences for working with large partitioned data sets, see .
taskbody.context: When the default settings for the MVS Files subsystem are in effect and a partitioned data set is expanded, the Remote Systems view displays a maximum of 50 partitioned data set members. You can take the following actions to browse through the members of a large partitioned data set.The examples use a smaller partitioned data set for purposes of illustration.
When you first expand a large partitioned data set, the members in excess of 50 are shown by using placeholders:Large partitioned data set placeholder

step.cmd: To show more members, double-click the placeholder.
step.stepresult: The next 50 members are displayed, and a placeholder is shown for the previous 50 members: Large partitioned data set with two placeholders

step.cmd: To continue to browse up and down through the members, double-click either the top or the bottom placeholders.
step.info: You can also browse through members by selecting a member name or placeholder and then pressing the keyboard shortcuts Ctrl+Home (top), Ctrl+End (bottom), Ctrl+PgUp (previous), or Ctrl+PgDn (next).

taskbody.context: The Locate menu item is also available on the view.

steps.stepsection: To locate a specific member name, do these steps:

step.cmd: Select the partitioned data set name, and then select Locate from the menu or press keyboard shortcut Ctrl+L.
In the Locate Member window, specify the member name that you want to locate and click OK.
step.info: You can specify a partial member name, such as MEM. If the name you search for does not exist, the member name just before the search string is located. Previous searches are preserved and added to a list in the Locate Member window. You can select a previous search string from this list.
step.stepresult: The member name is displayed and highlighted: Located partitioned data set member

task.title: Changing the number of members to display

steps-unordered.stepsection: To change the numbers of members to display for a partitioned data set, do one of the following actions:

step.cmd: Select a place holder and then select Show More or Show Less from the menu. A window opens prompting you to specify the number of members to display.
Select a partitioned data set name and then select Properties from the menu. On the Properties page, select Expansion and specify the number of members to display in the Expansion page size field.
